assessor_1_raw:
ASSESSMENT 1:
19.4% MAPE for low-volume stores and no override capability are both confirmed deployment concerns. Full-autonomy all-stores deployment requires modification. Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED.
assessor_2_raw:
ASSESSMENT 2:
Low-volume store performance gap is material for perishable autonomous ordering. No override creates systemic risk. Both require deployment design changes. Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED.
assessor_3_raw:
ASSESSMENT 3:
The model is appropriate for high-volume stores as deployed. Low-volume stores require either restricted deployment or performance validation at acceptable error levels. Override capability is a minimum safety requirement for perishable goods. Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED.
adjudication_raw: Unanimous empirical_test_agreed. Low-volume performance gap and no-override design both confirmed. Empirical test on production MAPE and waste/stockout rates specified.

empirical_test.measure: Production MAPE comparison between bottom-quintile and top-quintile stores in a 60-day pilot; perishable waste and stockout rate comparison across store tiers vs. rule-based baseline
empirical_test.success_criterion: Bottom-quintile production MAPE does not substantially exceed 19.4% holdout MAPE; waste and stockout rates for bottom-quintile stores within 2x of top-quintile rates
empirical_test.failure_criterion: Bottom-quintile production MAPE exceeds 25%; waste or stockout rates for bottom-quintile stores more than 2x top-quintile rates or substantially worse than rule-based baseline
